4864.history: Initially comissioned to the Baltic Fleet. Transferred to the North Fleet by river channels (Belomoro-Baltiyski channel) in May-August 1933 and joined the North Fleet on 5 August 1933. Modernized during 1936-37. Made 3 patrols during the Soviet-Finnish war (no successes). Lost during a training dive in Motovsky bay on 13 November 1940 with all hands. Possibly exceeded crush depth, but exact reason still not known.

4865.history: Baltic Fleet. Decommissioned on 20 June 1956. Stricken on 5 March 1987. Became a museum ship on 8 July 1989 at Saint Petersburg.

4866.history: Northern Fleet. Lost after 10 June 1942. Possibly due to mines. All hands lost.

4867.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely mined off the western-Crimea after 1 December 1943. Often reported as being sunk on 4 December 1943 by German auxiliary submarine chasers UJ-102, UJ-103, UJ-307, UJ-2305 and UJ-2308 near Cape Uret. But this attack was actually against Shch-209 lightly damaging this submarine.

4868.history: Black Sea fleet. Broken up at Sevastopol after 18 January 1956.

4869.history: Black Sea fleet. Destroyed and scuttled by her own crew in Sevastopol, due to the German advance on the city, while she was under repairs. Hull raised after the war and broken up.

4870.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned in August 1941. Stricken on 7 July 1945. Broken up for scrap in 1949.

4871.history: Baltic fleet. On 14 November 1941 L-2 hit a mine at 0107hours. She hit a second mine at 0110 hours. She was mined a third time at 0617 and sank off Keri Island in position 59.46N, 25.11E. 50 dead and 3 survivors.

4873.history: Black Sea fleet. Decommissioned on 2 November 1954. Stricken on 17 February 1956.

4874.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 29 December 1955.

4875.history: Black Sea fleet. Sunk in the Black Sea east of Constanta, Romania in position 43.28N, 31.32E by depth charges from the German auxiliary submarine chaser UJ 104.

4876.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4877.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4880.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zavod imeni Leninskogo Komsomola (Komsomolsk-na-Amur, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 199

4882.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4883.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4885.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
4885.history: Pacific fleet. L-16 left Petropavlovsk with her sister ship L-15 to join the Northen fleet on 26 September 1942. The two submarines intended to sail trough Dutch Harbour, San Francisco to the Panama Canal, Canada and the United Kingdom. L-16 was lost enroute due to the fact that she was torpedoed and sunk by the Japanese submarine I-25 (offsite link) on 11 October 1942 approximately 500 miles west of Seattle, Washington, U.S.A in position 45º41'N, 138º56W'. All 50 aboard were killed. The sinking was witnessed by the crew of L-15.

4886.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4887.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4888.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
4888.history: Pacific fleet. Lost on or after 24 August 1945 due to unknown causes. Probably mined in or off the La Pérouse Strait.

4890.history: Baltic fleet. Bombed by German aircraft at Leningrad before completion on 24 May 1942. Sunk in shallow water. Later raised and repaired.

4892.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely sunk north-west of Cape Tarchakut, Crimea by the German auxiliary submarine chaser UJ 106.

4893.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost between 15 and 29 December 1942 in a Bulgarian minefield off Cape Shabla in position 43.19.4N, 28.41.5E. All 60 men lost.

4894.history: Never completed. Evacuated incomplete from Nikolayev in mid-August 1941. Sunk on 18 December 1944 while under tow to Sevastopol. The boat was still not completed at that time.

4911.history: Pacific fleet. Sunk in a collission with M-12 on 1 June 1948. Raised an repaired. Stricken on 18 January 1951.

4915.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 6 October 1944 - 11 December 1944. Stricken on 31 May 1946.

4916.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 6 October 1944 - 11 December 1944. Stricken on 31 May 1946.

4917.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 30 August 1944 - 20 October 1944. Stricken on 31 May 1946.

4918.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 6 October 1944 - 11 December 1944. Stricken on 18 January 1951.

4919.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 6 October 1944 - 11 December 1944. Stricken on 31 May 1946.

4920.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 6 October 1944 - 11 December 1944. Stricken on 18 January 1951.

4921.history: Northern fleet. Transported from the Caspian Sea to the Arctic during 14 April / 28 May 1943. Sunk on 21 November 1956 in the Baltic Sea after a collision with a Russian destroyer.

4922.history: Northern fleet. Transported from the Caspian Sea to the Arctic during 14 April / 28 May 1943.

4923.history: Black Sea fleet. Accidentaly sunk on 22 September 1943 off Och'amch'ire, Georgia. Raised three days later. Decommissioned on 30 December 1944.

4924.history: Black Sea fleet >>> to Caspian Sea January 1943 >>> Returned to Black Sea in October 1943. Stricken on 20 July 1945.

4925.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-47 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4926.history: Black Sea fleet. Grounded on 10 December 1941 later salvaged and repaired. Decommissioned on 18 October 1944. Stricken on 19 December 1944.

4927.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 19 December 1944.

4928.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-48 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4929.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-49 on 27 October 1939. Most likely sunk in a Russian defensive minefield off Vladivostok between 10 and 16 August 1941.

4930.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely mined and sunk between 17 and 21 October 1941 in the Danube estuary area.

4931.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely mined and sunk in early November 1941 east of Constanza, Romania.

4932.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely mined and sunk near Odessa, Ukraine between 23/26 September 1942.

4934.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 17 August 1953.

4935.history: Pacific fleet. Most likely sunk in a Russian defensive minefield off Vladivostok between 10 and 16 August 1941.

4936.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4936.history: Baltic fleet. Scuttled at Libau to prevent capture.

4937.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4937.history: Baltic fleet. Hit a mine in the Gulf of Finland on 28 June 1941 and sustained heavy damage. Decommissioned to reserve in August 1941. Stricken on 2 December 1944.

4938.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4938.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned to reserve in August 1941. Stricken on 2 December 1944.

4939.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4939.history: Baltic fleet. Bombed and sunk at Kronstadt by German aircraft. Raised in 1942 but deemed not worth repairing. Stricken on 2 December 1944.

4940.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4940.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned to reserve in August 1941. Stricken on 2 December 1944.

4941.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4941.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned to reserve in 1941. Stricken on 2 December 1944.

4942.built_by: Sudomekh (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 196 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198
4942.history: Baltic fleet (also served on Lake Ladoga). Stricken on 22 February 1949.

4943.built_by: Sudomekh (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 196 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198
4943.history: Blatic fleet. Torpedoes and sunk by the German submarine U-144 west of Widawa in position 57.28N, 21.17E. Hit by U-boatSunk on 23 Jun 1941 by U-144 (Mittelstaedt).

4944.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4944.history: Baltic fleet (also served on Lake Ladoga). Stricken on 22 February 1949.

4945.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4945.history: Baltic fleet. Scuttled at Libau to prevent capture.

4946.history: Black Sea fleet. Went missing in the area off Fidonisi Island after 17 December 1942.

4947.history: Black Sea fleet >>> Caspian Sea (late 1942) >>> Black Sea fleet (late 1943). Stricken on 17 March 1953.

4948.history: Black Sea fleet. Mined and sunk near Odessa, Ukraine in position 46.20N, 30.54E between 22 August and 25 August 1942.

4949.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost near Constanza, Romania after 3 November 1941.

4950.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 20 June 1956.

4951.history: Black Sea fleet >>> to Caspian Sea in late 1942 / early 1943 >>> returned to Black Sea in late 1943. Lost in the morning 4 Janaury 1944 during post-repair sea trials off the Georgian coast. All 22 men aboard were lost.

4952.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-43 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4953.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-44 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4954.history: Pacific fleet. Remaned M-45 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4955.history: Pacific fleet. Renamed M-46 on 27 October 1939. Stricken on 28 November 1950.

4956.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4956.history: Mined and sunk off Vormsi, Estonia in position 59.09N, 22.58E.

4957.built_by: Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198 and Zhdanov (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 190
4957.history: Scuttled off Libau while returning to port on 27 June 1941. Was damaged on the 25th(???) by aircraft.

4958.history: Black Sea fleet. Transferred to Bulgaria on 03 September 1954. Returned to the USSR in 1958. Stricken on 20 February 1959.

4959.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned on 20 November 1952. Stricken on 26 June 1954.

4960.history: Baltic fleet. Torpedoed and sunk by German submarine U-140 in the Baltic Sea in position 58.51N, 22.02E. Hit by U-boatSunk on 21 Jul 1941 by U-140 (Hellriegel).

4961.history: Baltic fleet. Sunk by mine east of Suursaari Island in mid June 1942. M-95 was ordered, in her first war patrol, to perform reconaissance duties in the Suursaari island area, as part of the first soviet submarine offensive in 1942 in the Baltic Sea. She left Lavansaari island on the 14th of June. She failed to report and was declared missing next day.

4962.history: Baltic fleet. Mined and sunk in Narva Bay on or around 8 September 1944.

4963.history: Baltic fleet. Sunk by depth charges from the Finnish patrol boat VMV 5 off Suursaari Island, Finland. In 1990 the wreck was found in position 59.50N, 24.30E.

4964.history: Baltic fleet. Mined and sunk on 14 or 15 November 1941 in the Gulf of Finland.

4965.history: Baltic fleet. At 04.27 hours on 27 June 1941, the M-99 was hit by two torpedoes from the German submarine U-149 and sank immediately east of Dagö Island in position 59.20N, 21.12E.

4966.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 2 October 1954.

4968.history: Baltic fleet. Mined and sunk on or after 25 August 1941 in the Gulf of Finland north of Vormsi Island, Estonia.

4969.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. Stricken on 19 September 1960.

4970.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. Stricken on 10 June 1955.

4971.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. After a failed attack on the German auxiliary submarine chaser UJ 1217 M-106 was sunk by depth charges (73 dropped in total) and ramming near Vardö, Norway the German auxiliary submarine chasers UJ 1206, UJ 1212, UJ 1214 and UJ 1217. It was UJ 1217 that rammed M-106 after it surfaced.

4972.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. Stricken on 21 May 1956.

4973.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. M-108 failed to return from patrol (21 to 28 February 1944) off the Norwegian coast, she was probably sunk by a mine off the Kongsfjord.

4974.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 17 August 1953.

4975.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 21 February 1957.

4976.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
4976.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 5 June 1944 - 8 July 1944. Stricken on 17 August 1953.

4977.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
4977.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 5 June 1944 - 8 July 1944. Stricken on 2 October 1954.

4978.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
4978.history: Pacific fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Transported from the Pacific to the Black Sea during 5 June 1944 - 8 July 1944. Stricken on 20 June 1956.

4979.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198
4979.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 17 July 1953.

4980.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198
4980.history: Black Sea fleet. Sunk off Cape Burnas in position 45º53'N, 30º19'E by the Romanian minesweepers Sublocotenant Ghiculescu and Locotenant-Commandor Stihi Eugen on 1 October 1942.

4981.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198
4981.history: Northern fleet >>> Black Sea fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. Transported from the Northern fleet to the Black Sea fleet during 11 May 1944 - 8 June 1944. Stricken on 2 September 1953.

4982.built_by: Krasnoye Sormovo (Gorkiy, U.S.S.R) / Yard 112 and Marti Yard (Nikolayev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 198

4983.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. Mined and sunk off the Varangerfjord, Norway between 8 and 14 November 1942.

4984.history: Northern fleet. Completed at Molotovsk at Yard No.402. M-122 had left base on 12 May 1943. Most likely sunk on 14 May 1943 by German aircraft off the Rybachiy peninsula in position 69.56N, 32.53E. Might also have been mined.

4986.history: Northern fleet. Went missing in early October 1943 off the Verangerfjord. Most likely mined in a German defensive minefield. M-172 had departed base on 1 October 1943.

4987.history: Northern fleet. M-173 departed base on 6 August 1942 but she did not return as expected on 18 August. She was most likely mined and sunk off Vardo, Norway in this time period.

4988.history: Northern fleet. M-174 departed base on 14 October 1943 for a patrol of the Verangerfjord. She never returned and was most likely sunk after hitting a mine in a German defensive minefield.

4989.history: Northern Fleet. M-175 was torpedoed and sunk by the German submarine U-584 in the Barents Sea north of the Rybachiy peninsula in position 70.09N, 32.50E. Hit by U-boatSunk on 10 Jan 1942 by U-584 (Deecke).

4990.history: Northern fleet. M-176 was most likely mined on or around 3 July 1942 in a German defensive mine barrage off the Verangerfjord. She had departed base on 21 June and was not seen again.

4991.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
